title: Detected stack: Django 2.2.0 / Python, nginx TLS vhost, Dockerfile, empty k8s dir
notes: Python/Django web app. Manifests: requirements.txt (PyPI). Infra: Dockerfile at root, nginx/nginx.conf (TLS reverse proxy), k8s/ directory empty. App code in app/ (settings.py, views/search.py, templates/search_results.html). Check CVE feed for django@2.2.0, requests@2.19.0, pyyaml@5.3 (all end-of-life or known-vulnerable versions).
triage_notes: Informational stack inventory; no false-positive patterns apply.

title: Raw SQL string concatenation with request.GET input — SQL injection
evidence: cursor.execute("SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE name = '" + q + "'")
notes: q is taken directly from request.GET.get('q', '') on line 6 and concatenated into the SQL string; unauthenticated reachable.
triage_notes: String concatenation of request.GET['q'] into cursor.execute in a live view; no safe wrapper.

title: Template |safe filter on reflected user input — XSS
fix_recipe:
from django.utils.html import format_html, escape
comment = escape(request.POST["comment"])
# Or use format_html for structured HTML construction
notes: q is request.GET['q'] rendered with |safe, which disables auto-escaping; reflected XSS.
triage_notes: |safe applied directly to request.GET input in a production template; not a developer-controlled string.

title: Template |safe filter on DB row field — stored XSS
evidence:
{% for r in rows %}
  • {{ r.1|safe }}
    • {% endfor %}
fix_recipe:
from django.utils.html import format_html, escape
comment = escape(request.POST["comment"])
# Or use format_html for structured HTML construction
notes: r.1 is the 'name' column from the users table; rendering with |safe permits stored XSS if names contain HTML.
triage_notes: |safe applied to DB-sourced user name; data is not developer-controlled, stored XSS reachable.

title: User input reflected in <title> — XSS (context: HTML title)
fix_recipe:
from django.utils.html import format_html, escape
comment = escape(request.POST["comment"])
# Or use format_html for structured HTML construction
notes: Django auto-escape would normally protect this (no |safe here), but the surrounding template has |safe on the same q variable on line 6 so confirm escape state; listed at medium for defence-in-depth review. FP suspected: Django auto-escape is active here.
triage_notes: No |safe filter on this interpolation; Django template auto-escape neutralises HTML in title context.

title: Django DEBUG=True in only settings file
notes: DEBUG=True leaks stack traces, local variables, settings on error pages. No separate prod settings file detected.
triage_notes: File is sole settings.py (not settings_dev.py) with no env-based override; FP pattern does not apply.

title: Django SECRET_KEY hardcoded in settings
notes: Static SECRET_KEY compromises session, CSRF, and signed-cookie integrity; must come from environment.
triage_notes: Hardcoded literal in sole settings.py with no CI/test isolation; treated as production configuration.

title: Django ALLOWED_HOSTS wildcard
notes: Wildcard ALLOWED_HOSTS accepts any Host header, enabling host-header poisoning (cache poisoning, password-reset link poisoning). No specific fix recipe in django.md for ALLOWED_HOSTS.
triage_notes: Wildcard in sole settings.py; no environment split restricts hosts in production.

title: Django missing SECURE_* / HSTS / secure cookie settings
notes: settings.py defines no SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE, CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE, SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT, or SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS; all defaults are insecure. No direct Fix recipe block for this composite configuration.
triage_notes: settings.py contains only three lines; none of the SECURE_* hardening directives are set.

title: nginx ssl_protocols enables TLSv1 and TLSv1.1
notes: RFC 9325 deprecates TLS 1.0 and 1.1 (POODLE, BEAST, Lucky13 exposure).
triage_notes: Directive affirmatively allow-lists TLSv1 and TLSv1.1 (not a disable/exclude).

title: nginx ssl_ciphers uses legacy HIGH preset
notes: 'HIGH' preset still includes 3DES on older OpenSSL builds and lacks explicit AEAD preference. Replace with Mozilla Intermediate list.
triage_notes: HIGH preset is active configuration, not a comment; no explicit ECDHE/AEAD allow-list.

title: nginx server_tokens on — version disclosure
notes: Emits nginx version in Server response header and error pages; aids reconnaissance.
triage_notes: Explicit server_tokens on in live server block on port 443; version leak confirmed.

title: nginx TLS vhost missing Strict-Transport-Security header
notes: No add_header Strict-Transport-Security present in ssl server block (comment on line 12 acknowledges it). TLS downgrade/strip exposure.
triage_notes: ssl server block has no add_header Strict-Transport-Security; comment on line 12 confirms absence.

title: nginx missing X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, CSP, Referrer-Policy
notes: No X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, or Referrer-Policy headers set. Combined hardening gap.
triage_notes: No add_header directives for any security-header family in the TLS vhost block.

title: nginx location / lacks rate limiting
notes: No limit_req_zone/limit_req directives configured; Django app proxied unboundedly. The reference pattern targets explicit /login or /auth paths; this is a catch-all. No dedicated Fix recipe for generic location rate limiting.
triage_notes: Reference pattern scopes rate-limiting to /login,/auth,/api; this is a generic catch-all location /.

title: Dockerfile runs container as root (no USER instruction)
fix_recipe:
FROM node:20.12.2-alpine3.19@sha256:<digest>
WORKDIR /app
RUN addgroup -S appgroup && adduser -S appuser -G appgroup
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --omit=dev
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup . .
USER appuser
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
notes: No USER instruction anywhere in Dockerfile; container runs as UID 0. Comment at line 3 and 9 explicitly acknowledges this.
triage_notes: Single-stage Dockerfile with no USER directive; final CMD runs as root, not an intermediate builder stage.

title: Dockerfile FROM tag not pinned to digest
notes: Mutable tag python:3.11 can silently change on registry push; pin to immutable @sha256 digest.
triage_notes: FROM references mutable tag python:3.11 with no @sha256 digest suffix.

title: Dockerfile COPY without --chown
notes: COPY defaults ownership to root:root; if the image later switches to non-root USER, permissions may not be correct. Also applies to COPY . . on line 7. No distinct Fix recipe block for --chown alone; see Multi-stage recipe.
triage_notes: COPY defaults to root:root; no USER directive exists later so ownership mismatch is latent but untriggered.

title: Dockerfile CMD uses Django runserver (development server) as entrypoint
notes: runserver is not intended for production (no security hardening, single-threaded, auto-reload). Should use gunicorn/uwsgi behind nginx. No specific recipe in django.md for WSGI server selection.
triage_notes: Dockerfile CMD invokes manage.py runserver directly; no gunicorn/uwsgi wrapper detected.

title: Django 2.2.0 is end-of-life (EOL April 2022)
notes: Django 2.2 LTS reached end-of-life April 2022; no security patches. Multiple known CVEs (SQLi, DoS, XSS) apply. Check CVE feed for django@2.2.0.
triage_notes: requirements.txt pins django==2.2.0, an EOL version; no override to a supported release observed.

title: requests 2.19.0 is outdated (released 2018)
notes: Check CVE feed for requests@2.19.0 — known issues include CVE-2018-18074 (Authorization header leakage via redirect) and CVE-2023-32681 (proxy auth leak). No in-repo reference file for requests.
triage_notes: requirements.txt pins requests==2.19.0; no alternative constraint elsewhere in repo.

title: pyyaml 5.3 pinned (yaml.load without SafeLoader risk)
notes: pyyaml 5.3 pinned; CVE-2020-14343 affects <5.4 when yaml.load is used without SafeLoader. No yaml.load call found in target; risk is latent. Check CVE feed for pyyaml@5.3.
triage_notes: pyyaml pinned but no yaml.load() call in target code; risk is latent, not reachable.
